spec-022-canvas-overlay-badges | Per-event SVG badges overlaid on LabwareCanvas | pending | ## Task Summary --- id: spec-021-tri-state-per-event-preview title: "Tri-state per-event preview state and sidebar controls" priority: 21 depends_on: - spec-020-fix-preview-render-gates acceptance_criteria: - "useAiChat exposes a new state Map called previewEventStates that is initialized to 'pending' for every event in a new previewEvents batch" - "useAiChat exposes setPreviewEventState(eventId, state) for toggling individual events between the three states" - "useAiChat exposes commitAcceptedPreviewEvents(): applies every event currently in state='accepted' to the editor via onAcceptEvent, then clears previewEvents, previewLabwareAdditions, previewEventStates. Pending and rejected events are discarded at commit time" - "useAiChat retains existing acceptPreview (applies ALL events) and rejectPreview (discards ALL) for the global Accept All / Reject All buttons — backward compatible" - "PreviewEventList.tsx: each event card now shows three controls: ✓ (Accept), ✗ (Reject), ○ (Reset to Pending). The current state is visually indicated by a border/background color on the card" - "PreviewBanner.tsx: alongside the existing Accept All / Reject All buttons, a new 'Commit Accepted (N)' button appears that calls commitAcceptedPreviewEvents and shows the count of events in state='accepted'. The button is disabled when no events are in accepted state" - "Rejecting an event visually hides it from the canvas — the ghost well is removed from previewWellContents. The state tracking remains so the user can toggle back to pending" - "DualLabwarePane's sourcePreviewWells and targetPreviewWells computations now filter by event state, excluding any event where previewEventStates.get(eventId) === 'rejected'" - "Unit tests at app/src/shared/hooks/useAiChat.triState.test.ts cover: (a) setting and getting event state, (b) commitAcceptedPreviewEvents applies only accepted events, (c) toggling rejection removes the event from preview maps but retains state, (d) new previewEvents batch resets all states to pending" - "A PreviewEventList visual test at app/src/shared/ai/PreviewEventList.triState.test.tsx asserts the three buttons render per event and clicking each invokes the corresponding handler" - "npx tsc --noEmit passes from app/" estimated_iterations: 6 --- # Tri-state per-event preview state and sidebar controls ## Context Today the AI preview flow is all-or-nothing: click Accept and all proposed events commit; click Reject and all are discarded. Brad wants per-event granularity with tri-state (pending → accepted/rejected → commit). This spec adds the state machine and the sidebar controls. Spec-022 adds the canvas overlay controls that reuse the same state. **State semantics:** - **pending** (default) — visible as dashed purple ghost (today's behavior), not yet decided - **accepted** — visible as dashed green ghost, queued for commit - **rejected** — hidden from canvas (matches Brad's "remove" intent), but state retained so user can toggle back - **commit** — apply all accepted, discard pending and rejected ## Requirements 1. **Extend `useAiChat.ts` state:** ```ts const [previewEventStates, setPreviewEventStatesMap] = useState>(new Map()); function setPreviewEventState(eventId: string, state: 'pending' | 'accepted' | 'rejected') { setPreviewEventStatesMap((prev) => { const next = new Map(prev); next.set(eventId, state); return next; }); } ``` On receipt of a new done event (the existing handler around line 205-229), reset the state map: ```ts const nextStates = new Map(); (result.events ?? []).forEach((e: PlateEvent) => { nextStates.set(e.eventId, 'pending'); }); setPreviewEventStatesMap(nextStates); ``` Expose `previewEventStates`, `setPreviewEventState`, and `commitAcceptedPreviewEvents` via the hook return object. 2. **Implement `commitAcceptedPreviewEvents`:** ```ts const commitAcceptedPreviewEvents = useCallback(async () => { const toApply = previewEvents.filter( (e) => previewEventStates.get(e.eventId) === 'accepted', ); // If there are any labware additions, apply them first (mirroring // acceptPreview's behavior). Use the same onAddLabwareFromRecord // callback path. Note: labware additions are NOT per-event in this // spec — they're still batch-committed. A future spec can add // per-addition granularity if needed. if (previewLabwareAdditions.length > 0) { // reuse the existing labware apply logic from acceptPreview } for (const event of toApply) { onAcceptEventRef.current?.(event); } // Clear preview state entirely on commit — pending and rejected // events are discarded. This matches the "commit is final" intent. setPreviewEvents([]); setPreviewLabwareAdditions([]); setPreviewEventStatesMap(new Map()); setMessages((prev) => [ ...prev, { id: `sys-${Date.now()}`, role: 'system', content: `Committed ${toApply.length} event${toApply.length === 1 ? '' : 's'}.`, isStreaming: false, }, ]); }, [previewEvents, previewEventStates, previewLabwareAdditions]); ``` 3. **Keep existing `acceptPreview` and `rejectPreview` unchanged** — they're the global "Accept All" and "Reject All" buttons. `acceptPreview` applies every event regardless of state; `rejectPreview` clears everything. 4. **DualLabwarePane filtering.** In `app/src/graph/labware/DualLabwarePane.tsx` at the two useMemo blocks (lines ~292-337 per prior exploration), add a filter step that excludes events with state === 'rejected'. The hook must pass `previewEventStates` down to DualLabwarePane, or DualLabwarePane must read it from context / a new prop. Simpler path: add a new prop `previewEventStates?: Map` and filter `previewEvents` at the top of each useMemo: ```ts const visiblePreviewEvents = previewEvents.filter( (e) => (previewEventStates?.get(e.eventId) ?? 'pending') !== 'rejected', ); // ... then compute sourcePreviewWells from visiblePreviewEvents ``` Wire the prop from the page-level component that renders DualLabwarePane — it's in LabwareEventEditor.tsx, probably around line 900ish where useAiChat is consumed. **Do NOT refactor DualLabwarePane's overall structure.** Minimal change: one new prop, one filter step per useMemo. 5. **PreviewEventList.tsx per-event controls.** In `app/src/shared/ai/PreviewEventList.tsx`, extend each event card with three buttons: ```tsx
``` Visual state: highlight the card border based on current state — gray for pending, green for accepted, red/faded for rejected. Keep the existing "Focus" button that highlights wells on canvas. The component now needs new props `previewEventStates`, `setPreviewEventState`. Pass them from AiChatPanel (which gets them from `useAiChat`). 6. **PreviewBanner 'Commit Accepted' button.** In `PreviewBanner.tsx`, add a third button alongside Accept All / Reject All: ```tsx ``` `acceptedCount` is computed from `previewEventStates` — count of entries whose value is 'accepted'. Pass it and `onCommitAccepted` as new props from AiChatPanel. 7. **AiChatPanel wiring.** In `app/src/shared/ai/AiChatPanel.tsx` (around lines 284-296 per prior exploration), pass the new props from `chat` (the useAiChat hook return) down to both PreviewEventList and PreviewBanner. 8. **Tests:** - `useAiChat.triState.test.ts`: - Mock the chat hook setup with a canned previewEvents array of 3 events - Assert all 3 start as 'pending' - Call setPreviewEventState(e1, 'accepted'), setPreviewEventState(e2, 'rejected') - Call commitAcceptedPreviewEvents - Assert onAcceptEvent was called exactly once (for e1) - Assert previewEvents is now [] - Assert previewEventStates is now empty - Separately: call setPreviewEventState to toggle an event back to pending from rejected - `PreviewEventList.triState.test.tsx`: - Render with 2 events and a fake setPreviewEventState spy - Query for the three buttons on the first card via aria-label - Click ✓ — assert spy called with ('e1', 'accepted') - Click ✗ — assert spy called with ('e1', 'rejected') - Click ○ — assert spy called with ('e1', 'pending') ## Files Likely Involved - `app/src/shared/hooks/useAiChat.ts` (modify) - `app/src/shared/ai/PreviewEventList.tsx` (modify) - `app/src/shared/ai/PreviewBanner.tsx` (modify — add commit button) - `app/src/shared/ai/AiChatPanel.tsx` (modify — wire new props) - `app/src/graph/labware/DualLabwarePane.tsx` (modify — filter by state) - `app/src/graph/LabwareEventEditor.tsx` (modify — pass new prop to DualLabwarePane) - `app/src/shared/hooks/useAiChat.triState.test.ts` (new) - `app/src/shared/ai/PreviewEventList.triState.test.tsx` (new) ## Verification ```bash cd app npx tsc --noEmit npx vitest run src/shared/hooks/useAiChat.triState.test.ts npx vitest run src/shared/ai/PreviewEventList.triState.test.tsx grep -n "previewEventStates" src/shared/hooks/useAiChat.ts grep -n "commitAcceptedPreviewEvents" src/shared/hooks/useAiChat.ts grep -n "Commit Accepted" src/shared/ai/PreviewBanner.tsx ``` Expected: typecheck passes, both tests pass, all greps succeed. ## Notes - **Do not run `pnpm test` or `pnpm lint`.** - Tri-state is client-side only. The server's AgentResult shape is unchanged. - Do not modify the PlateEvent type. - Rejected events are hidden from the canvas but retained in state so users can undo. The Commit action is what permanently discards them. - The global Accept All / Reject All buttons remain for bulk actions. - Canvas overlay badges (spec-022) will reuse the same `previewEventStates` state machine — don't fork it. ## Non-Negotiable Invariants - Three distinct states: pending, accepted, rejected. - Rejected events do not appear in `previewWellContents` on the canvas. - `commitAcceptedPreviewEvents` only applies events in state='accepted'. - Committing clears all preview state (pending and rejected are discarded at commit time). - Existing acceptPreview / rejectPreview bulk actions still work. ## Dependencies and Tooling None. ## Negative Cases - Must NOT change the server-side AgentResult. - Must NOT change PlateEvent. - Must NOT delete rejected events from useState immediately — retain the tri-state entry so the user can toggle back. - Must NOT commit pending events on commit — only accepted. ## Repo Commands - install: pnpm install - lint: pnpm lint - test: pnpm test - build: pnpm build - command runner: /home/brad/git/agent-workbench/scripts/run-repo-command.sh ## Retrieval Highlights - docs/adr-event-vignette-authoring-v2.md:6-36 score=3.073744 reason=token overlap=45, path match, heading match symbol=Context - docs/material-concentration-migration-checklist.md:223-239 score=2.247688 reason=token overlap=28, path match, heading match symbol=Phase 7: Scientific Display In Tooltips And Context Panels - docs/material-concentration-migration-checklist.md:240-253 score=2.156652 reason=token overlap=27, path match, heading match symbol=Phase 8: AI Context And End-To-End Planning - docs/adr-run-workspace-centered-ai-thin-contract.md:115-125 score=2.000349 reason=token overlap=23, path match, heading match symbol=1. It is available as an MCP server — you can call its tools directly. ## When to use - The app is running and you need to verify UI behavior - You need to test user flows (login, form submission, navigation) - You want visual verification of rendered output ## When NOT to use - No app is configured or the app is not running - The task is backend-only with no UI component ## Available tools | Tool | Purpose | |------|---------| | `browser_navigate` | Navigate to a URL | | `browser_snapshot` | Get the current page's accessibility snapshot (DOM structure) | | `browser_click` | Click an element by CSS selector or text | | `browser_fill` | Fill a form input field | | `browser_type` | Type text character by character | | `browser_select_option` | Select from a dropdown | | `browser_press_key` | Press a keyboard key | | `browser_take_screenshot` | Capture a screenshot for visual verification | | `browser_hover` | Hover over an element | | `browser_wait_for` | Wait for an element or condition | ## Example workflow 1.
